name: Veal Dijon
recipeInstructions:
The cut of veal you use will affect cooking time. If using stew meat, plan for up to 1 hour of stewing in its juices. For medallions or better cuts, it cooks quickly.
1. In a heavy-bottomed pan, sauté butter and shallots.
2. Add the veal (medallions) and cook until browned (about 5 minutes).
3. Remove meat from the pan and set aside.
4. Deglaze the pan with white wine.
5. Mix mustard and honey in a separate bowl.
6. Gradually add cream, reducing to desired thickness.
7. Season sauce with salt and pepper.
8. Return meat to the pan; heat together until serving.
9. Serves 2
